ORDER ON DEFENDANT BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO MARIA CHAVARRIA IS DENIED AND THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT BREACHED PARAGRAPHS 2 AND 3 OF THE DEED OF TRUST BY ALLOWING THE STATE FARM POLICY TO LAPSE AND/OR BE CANCELLED WITHOUT NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. CAL. INS. CODE 673 DOES NOT APPLY TO THIS CASE. DEFENDANT'S DUTY TO PLAINTIFF ARISES FROM CONTRACT AND THERE IS NO INDEPENDENT TORT DUTY THAT DEFENDANT BREACHED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE INSURANCE POLICY UNDER EVIDENCE CODE 452(H). EVEN IF PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THAT THEY ARE INTENDED THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARIES OF THE POLICY, PARAGRAPH 11 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFFS WERE ADDED AS ADDITIONAL INSUREDS PURSUANT TO AN ENDORSEMENT. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFFS HAVE STANDING TO MAINTAIN THIS ACTION. (COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER MIRRORING THE COURT'S RULING AND EMAIL TO COURT- COPYING OPPOSING COUNSEL- FOR SIGNATURE). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. (CCP 430.10(E).) EACH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUFFICIENTLY PLED. FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, WHETHER BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICES ("BAYVIEW") HAS SUFFERED DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT INVOLVES A QUESTION OF FACT THAT IS NOT THE PROPER SUBJECT OF A DEMURRER. FOR THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, BAYVIEW HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE AN ACTUAL AND PRESENT CONTROVERSY OVER THE INSURANCE POLICY PROVISIONS. FOR THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY ("GREAT AMERICAN") PREDICATES ITS DEMURRER ON THE FAILURE OF THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIMS. FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, BAYVIEW HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR IMPLIED CONTRACTUAL INDEMNITY. (CACI NO. 3801; WEST V. SUPERIOR COURT (1994) 27 CAL.APP.4TH 1625, 1633.) PLAINTIFFS' DISMISSAL, WITH PREJUDICE, OF GREAT AMERICAN IS NOT DISPOSITIVE TO THIS CROSS-ACTION. (JOHNSON V. COUNTY OF FRESNO (2003) 111 CAL.APP.4TH 1087, 1096.) THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBITS A - D ARE GRANTED AS TO THEIR FILING, BUT NOT AS TO THE TRUTH OF THE MATTERS ASSERTED THEREIN. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBIT E IS GRANTED AS THE TRUSTEE'S DEED UPON SALE IS RECORDED AND THE FACTS ARE NOT DISPUTED. (FONTENOT V. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. (2011) 198 CAL.APP.4TH 256, 265.) THE ADDITIONAL REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBIT A, THE INSURANCE POLICY, IS GRANTED AS THE POLICY IS NOT REASONABLY SUBJECT TO DISPUTE. (EVID. CODE 452(H).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
ORDER RE: GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE CYNTHIA M. LEE; Clerk KCox; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-18-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-22-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DANIEL FOSTER, ESQ., 916.441.2430, FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING.
WILLIAM HAPIUK, ESQ., 415.365.9804, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS-DEFENDANT GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is Denied. Bayview Loan Servicing has adequately pled facts to support its punitive damage claims. (Civ. Code 3294.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DANIEL FOSTER, ESQ., 916.441.2430, FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING.
WILLIAM HAPIUK, ESQ., 415.365.9804, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the first, second, third and fourth causes of action. (CCP 430.10(e).) Each cause of action is sufficiently pled. For the first cause of action, whether Bayview Loan Services ("Bayview") has suffered damages for breach of contract involves a question of fact that is not the proper subject of a demurrer. For the second cause of action, Bayview has pled sufficient facts to allege an actual and present controversy over the insurance policy provisions. For the third cause of action, Great American Assurance Company ("Great American") predicates its demurrer on the failure of the breach of contract claims. For the fourth cause of action, Bayview has pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for implied contractual indemnity. (CACI no. 3801; West v. Superior Court (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 1625, 1633.) Plaintiffs' dismissal, with prejudice, of Great American is not dispositive to this cross-action. (Johnson v. County of Fresno (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 1087, 1096.) The request for judicial notice of Exhibits A - D are granted as to their filing, but not as to the truth of the matters asserted therein. The request for judicial notice of Exhibit E is granted as the Trustee's Deed Upon Sale is recorded and the facts are not disputed. (Fontenot v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 256, 265.) The additional request for judicial notice of Exhibit A, the insurance policy, is granted as the policy is not reasonably subject to dispute. (Evid. Code 452(h).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash The Deposition Subpoena Of The Custodian Of Records And The Deposition Subpoena Of Person Most Knowledgeable
Off Calendar Oct-03-2013 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. J. EDWARD KERLEY OF THE HEREFORD/KERLEY LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 379-5801 EXT. 210 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NATALIE P. VANCE OF KLINEDINST, TEL. NO. (916) 444-7573 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO JUAN CHAVARRIA is denied and the Court denies summary adjudication on the breach of contract cause of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant breached paragraphs 2 and 3 of the Deed of Trust by allowing the State Farm policy to lapse and/or be cancelled without notice to plaintiff. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the negligence cause of action. Cal. Ins. Code 673 does not apply to independent tort duty that defendant breached. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/PJB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. J. EDWARD KERLEY OF THE HEREFORD/KERLEY LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 379-5801 EXT. 210 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NATALIE P. VANCE OF KLINEDINST, TEL. NO. (916) 444-7573 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO MARIA CHAVARRIA is denied and the Court denies summary adjudication on the breach of contract cause of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant breached paragraphs 2 and 3 of the Deed of Trust by allowing the State Farm policy to lapse and/or be cancelled without notice to plaintiff. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the negligence cause of action. Cal. Ins. Code 673 does not apply to this case. Defendant's duty to plaintiff arises from contract and there is no independent tort duty that defendant breached. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/PJB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, LLC's Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint is Granted. The proposed cross-complaint arises out of the same transaction as plaintiff's claim. Defendant Bayview Loan Servicing Inc. did not act in bad faith in seeking to file a cross-complaint at this time, and the filing of the cross-complaint is warranted in the interests of justice. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Off Calendar Nov-15-2012 Continued To Jan-30-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: GENE HALAVANAU, ESQ., HEREFORD KERLEY LLP, 510.379.5801 X203, FOR PLAINTIFFS JUAN AND MARIA CHAVARRIA.
PETER WHALEN, ESQ., CLYDE & CO US LLP, 415.365.9800, FOR DEFENDANT GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT GREAT AMERICAN ASSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The Court takes judicial notice of the insurance policy under Evidence Code 452(h). Even if plaintiffs failed to plead facts showing that they are intended third party beneficiaries of the policy, paragraph 11 of the amended complaint alleges that plaintiffs were added as additional insureds pursuant to an endorsement. Accordingly, plaintiffs have standing to maintain this action. (counsel for plaintiff to prepare a form of order mirroring the court's ruling and email to court- copying opposing counsel- for signature). Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
